reasoning: The document suffers from major structural defects, most notably the duplication of the entire resolution content and the corruption of the signature block into a table with fabricated headers. While the programmatic word count ratio is perfect, the fidelity score of 0.6886 and the identified structural and reading order issues fall significantly below the threshold for auto-approval.

concerns.analysis: The signature block at the bottom of the document is represented as a table (reading_order 12). This table uses fabricated headers ('Field' and 'Value') that do not exist in the source. Furthermore, it collapses the side-by-side (2-column) layout of the original signatures into a single column (names/titles are both in the first column), which is a corruption of the document's logical structural organization for these elements.

concerns.output_quote: Table element at index 12 with rows containing names in one column and empty strings in the second, plus fabricated 'Field'/'Value' headers.

concerns.analysis: The Mitchell County seal image, which is positioned at the top center of the source document header, has been moved to the end of the document in the reading order (element 13, following the text body and signature table). This is a material change to the logical flow of the document.
